title: Elliptic Curve Cryptography
text: Elliptic Curve Cryptography Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) is an approach for asymmetric key cryptography based on the algebraic structure of elliptic curves over finite fields. You can use Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) for encryption and digital signatures. ECC provides strong security with relatively smaller keys compared to non-ECC. ECC is well-suited for a wide range of applications in the field of information security and secure communication. Encryption and Signature Schemes Table 1. Encryption and Signature Schemes Name Usage Description Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme (ECIES) Used for encryption A hybrid encryption system. Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) Used for signature signing and verification A variant of the Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA) which uses elliptic-curve cryptography. Ed25519 Used for signature signing and verification A deterministic signature scheme using curve25519. Supported ECC Algorithms Table 2. Supported ECC Algorithms Algorithm Meaning ECIES_ECP_SHA256 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-256 hash function ECIES_ECP_SHA384 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-384 hash function ECIES_ECP_SHA512 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-512 hash function ECIES_ECP_SHA3-256 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-256 hash function ECIES_ECP_SHA3-384 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-384 hash function ECIES_ECP_SHA3-512 Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-512 hash function ED25519 EdDSA signature scheme using SHA-512 and curve25519 ECDSA_ECP_SHA256 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-256 hash function ECDSA_ECP_SHA384 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-384 hash function ECDSA_ECP_SHA512 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA-512 hash function ECDSA_ECP_SHA3-256 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-256 hash function ECDSA_ECP_SHA3-384 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-384 hash function ECDSA_ECP_SHA3-512 Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm using elliptic curves over prime fields with SHA3-512 hash function Generating Keys Uniface accepts the PKCS #8 private key format and X.509 public key format. For interoperability, PEM (Privacy Enhanced Mail) encoding is used for both keys. PEM encoding is a commonly used Base64 encoding with a header and a footer. You can generate private and public keys using OpenSSL. For example, the following command generates a private key for curve prime256v1 and saves it in a file named myPrivateKey.pem : Explain this code # find your curve openssl ecparam -list_curves # generate a private key for a curve openssl ecparam -name prime256v1 -genkey -noout -out prime256v1.pem # format it to pkcs#8 openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -in prime256v1.pem -inform pem -nocrypt -out myPrivateKey.pem -outform pem Copy To generate the corresponding public key, use the following command: Explain this code # generate corresponding public key openssl ec -in myPrivateKey.pem -pubout -out myPublicKey.pem Copy For more information, see OpenSSL documentation . Example: Encrypting and Decrypting Data Using Keys Using the $encode and $decode ProcScript functions, you can use keys to encrypt and decrypt data. The key needs to be loaded into a string. Uniface accepts the key parameter as string as shown in the following example: Explain this code variables String vMyPrivateKey, vMyPublicKey Raw plaintext,ciphertext endvariables ; Load keys fileload "myPrivateKey.pem", vMyPrivateKey fileload "myPublicKey.pem", vMyPublicKey ; Encrypt data by ECIES ECP with SHA-512 hash ciphertext = $encode("ECIES_ECP_SHA512", plaintext, vMyPublicKey) ; Decrypt the ciphertext plaintext = $decode("ECIES_ECP_SHA512", ciphertext, vMyPrivateKey) Copy Example: Signing and Verifying Signatures Using $encode and $decode, you can sign and verify a message with a digital signature: Explain this code variables String vMyPrivateKey, vMyPublicKey, vMessage Raw vSignature endvariables ; Sign a message by ECDSA ECP with SHA512 hash. vSignature = $encode("ECDSA_ECP_SHA512, vMessage, vMyPrivateKey) ; Verify the signed message. if ($decode("ECDSA_ECP_SHA512", vMessage, vMyPublicKey, vSignature)) message/info "This is a valid message." else message/info "Signature doesn’t match with the message." endif Copy Was this topic helpful?
